---
title: "4.8 ArrayList Methods"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### ArrayLists resize as elements are added and removed

![Lists](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-8-arraylist-methods/lists.jpg){fig-align="center" width="36%"}

An <span class="term">ArrayList</span> stores objects in order and can change size during runtime.

You must import it:

```java
import java.util.ArrayList;
```

## Declaring and Creating

### Use a type argument

```java
ArrayList<String> names = new ArrayList<String>();
ArrayList<Integer> scores = new ArrayList<Integer>();
```

AP CSA uses generic type arguments such as `<String>` or `<Integer>` to describe the element type.

## `size()`

### Number of elements currently stored

```java
ArrayList<String> names = new ArrayList<String>();
names.add("Ana");
names.add("Bo");
System.out.println(names.size());
```

Output:

```text
2
```

Unlike arrays, `ArrayList` uses `size()` as a method.

## `add(obj)`

### Append to the end

```java
names.add("Cam");
names.add("Dia");
```

The list grows as new elements are appended.

For `ArrayList<Integer>`, Java autoboxes primitive `int` values into `Integer` objects.

## `add(index, obj)`

### Insert and shift later elements right

```java
ArrayList<String> letters = new ArrayList<String>();
letters.add("A");
letters.add("C");
letters.add(1, "B");
```

Result:

```text
[A, B, C]
```

## `remove(index)`

### Remove and shift later elements left

```java
String removed = letters.remove(1);
```

After removing index `1` from `[A, B, C]`, the list becomes `[A, C]`.

The method returns the removed object.

## `get` and `set`

### Read or replace one element

```java
String first = letters.get(0);
letters.set(1, "D");
```

::: {.tight-list}
- `get(index)` returns the element
- `set(index, obj)` replaces the element and returns the old value
:::

## Arrays versus ArrayLists

### Fixed size versus dynamic size

| Feature | Array | ArrayList |
|---|---|---|
| size | fixed | can change |
| length | `.length` | `.size()` |
| element access | `arr[i]` | `list.get(i)` |
| replace | `arr[i] = x` | `list.set(i, x)` |

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### FRQ Digits

Build an `ArrayList<Integer>` containing the digits of a positive integer.

```java
ArrayList<Integer> digits = new ArrayList<Integer>();
int number = 1234;

while (number > 0)
{
    digits.add(0, number % 10);
    number /= 10;
}
```

Trace why inserting at index `0` keeps the digits in normal order.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- import and create an `ArrayList` with a type argument
- use `size()`, `add`, `remove`, `get`, and `set`
- explain how insertions and removals shift elements
- distinguish array syntax from `ArrayList` method calls
- use `ArrayList<Integer>` for integer collections
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
